findings.description: The NCDOR logo in the HTML version appears slightly different in styling/rendering compared to the PDF version
findings.evidence: PDF shows NCDOR logo with 'Web 12-17' beneath it in a more compact header layout; HTML shows the logo with 'Web' and '12-17' as separate lines below it

findings.description: The header layout differs - PDF has the title and logo side by side with a vertical bar separator, HTML stacks them vertically
findings.evidence: PDF: NCDOR logo | GAS-1264 Instructions title side by side. HTML: Logo on top, title below with horizontal rule separator

findings.description: List item formatting differs slightly - PDF uses indented numbered list with periods, HTML uses a different numbering style
findings.evidence: PDF: '1.' with tab indent. HTML: '1.' with standard list formatting

findings.description: Line-by-line instructions use different dash styles for line labels
findings.evidence: PDF uses 'Line 1 - Beginning physical inventory' with bold formatting; HTML uses 'Line 1 - Beginning physical inventory' similarly but dash style differs slightly

findings.description: The numbered/lettered sub-items under Line 18 Administrative Discount use slightly different formatting
findings.evidence: PDF uses indented numbered list with sub-letters a), b), c); HTML preserves the same structure with minor style differences

findings.description: The column instructions section for disbursement schedules has slightly different spacing between column definitions
findings.evidence: PDF shows Column 3 Mode transport types in a tabular inline layout (J=Truck, R=Rail, etc.); HTML shows them in a similar but slightly reformatted layout

findings.description: The MAIL TO and QUESTIONS sections at the bottom of the last page maintain the two-column layout in both versions
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show the mailing address on the left and contact information on the right

findings.description: The HTML has a nested <main> element inside another <main> element, which is invalid HTML structure. The header and footer are also placed inside the outer <main>. However, the heading hierarchy itself is reasonable: h1 for the title, h2 for major sections, h3 for sub-sections, and h4 for line-by-line items.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main> wrapping a <header>, another <main>, and <footer>. This is a structural HTML issue but does not affect content fidelity.

findings.description: The PDF references www.ncdor.gov as hyperlinks in multiple places, but the HTML renders them as bold text rather than actual clickable hyperlinks (<a> tags).
findings.evidence: PDF: 'the rates should be verified on the Department's website at www.ncdor.gov' appears as a link. HTML: rendered as www.ncdor.gov without an tag.

findings.description: The receipts schedule column instructions reference 'Columns 5 & 6: Seller' in the PDF, but the HTML renders this as 'Columns 5 & 6: Seller' correctly. However, the disbursement column instructions skip Column 5 in the PDF (going from Column 4 to Columns 6 & 7), and the HTML correctly reflects this.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML correctly show the column numbering differences between receipt and disbursement schedules.

findings.description: The Schedule 8 instruction references 'Part, 2, Line 19' in the PDF (with an extra comma), and the HTML faithfully reproduces this typo from the source document.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'should be included on Part, 2, Line 19 of the GAS-1264 return.' HTML: 'should be included on Part, 2, Line 19 of the GAS-1264 return.'
